remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Migraine frequency fluctuates; spontaneous remission (no attacks for 12+ months) occurs in ~25% over 10-year follow-up; 40% of migraine sufferers qualify for preventive therapy but only 3-13% receive it
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: CGRP monoclonal antibodies (erenumab, fremanezumab, galcanezumab) achieve ≥50% reduction in monthly migraine days in 50-70% of patients; complete response (near-zero days) in 10-30%; acute triptans achieve 2-hour pain freedom in 30-40%
remission.gap_size: Large — primarily an access/utilization gap for preventive therapy
remission.barrier_detail: Only 3-13% of the ~10 million US adults who qualify for preventive therapy receive it; prior authorization barriers for CGRP mAbs are substantial; widespread underdiagnosis; opioid prescribing for migraine in ED associated with medication overuse headache
remission.last_soc_change: 2018-2020 — FDA approved first CGRP-targeted preventive therapies (erenumab 2018, fremanezumab/galcanezumab 2018-2019, atogepant 2021); prior prevention (beta-blockers, topiramate, amitriptyline) unchanged since 1990s-2000s; triptans first-line acute since 1993
remission.outdated_treatments: Opioids for acute migraine (associated with medication overuse headache, MOH, in 50-70% of frequent users; actually worsens long-term migraine frequency); opioids in ED for acute migraine (inferior to IV prochlorperazine + diphenhydramine, yet remain common); barbiturate-containing analgesics (Fioricet/butalbital — MOH risk, no superiority over NSAIDs)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Magnesium IV/oral (effective for acute migraine and prevention; dramatically underused); riboflavin 400mg/day (preventive; NNT ~3 in RCTs); CoQ10 300mg/day (Cochrane-reviewed); neuromodulation devices (Cefaly transcranial supraorbital stimulation, FDA cleared; gammaCore vagus nerve stimulation)
remission.frontier_signal: Gepants (oral CGRP receptor antagonists — ubrogepant, rimegepant — acute and preventive dual role); lasmiditan (5-HT1F agonist without vasoconstriction, for cardiovascular-contraindicated patients); psilocybin for cluster headache (NIH-funded Phase 2 underway); transcranial magnetic stimulation (SpringTMS, FDA cleared)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
